title: NPC System — Scripted Characters
description: Create an NPC class with name, dialogue (dict mapping keyword to response string), inventory (list of item dicts with 'name' and 'price'), and is_merchant (bool). Create an NPCManager that loads NPC definitions from a list of dicts. Method: interact(npc, keyword) returns the matching dialogue response or a default message. Merchants support buy(npc, item_name, player_gold) and sell(npc, item_name) with price checking.
test_code:
# Tests for Step 7: NPC System
assert 'NPC' in dir(), 'NPC class must be defined'
assert 'NPCManager' in dir(), 'NPCManager class must be defined'

# Test NPC constructor
npc = NPC('Guard', {'hello': 'Welcome to town!', 'quest': 'Clear the rats from the cellar.'})
assert npc.name == 'Guard', 'NPC should store name'
assert npc.dialogue == {'hello': 'Welcome to town!', 'quest': 'Clear the rats from the cellar.'}, 'NPC should store dialogue'
assert npc.inventory == [], 'Default inventory should be empty list'
assert npc.is_merchant is False, 'Default is_merchant should be False'

# Test NPC defaults
npc2 = NPC('Villager')
assert npc2.dialogue == {}, 'Default dialogue should be empty dict'
assert npc2.inventory == [], 'Default inventory should be empty list'

# Test get_response
assert npc.get_response('hello') == 'Welcome to town!', 'Should return matching dialogue'
assert npc.get_response('quest') == 'Clear the rats from the cellar.', 'Should return quest dialogue'
default_resp = npc.get_response('nonsense')
assert 'nothing to say' in default_resp.lower() or 'Guard' in default_resp, f'Unknown keyword should give default response, got: {default_resp}'

# Test merchant NPC
merchant = NPC(
    'Shopkeeper',
    {'hello': 'Browse my wares!'},
    [{'name': 'health potion', 'price': 50}, {'name': 'iron sword', 'price': 200}],
    is_merchant=True
)
assert merchant.is_merchant is True, 'Shopkeeper should be a merchant'
assert len(merchant.inventory) == 2, 'Shopkeeper should have 2 items'

# Test get_item
potion = merchant.get_item('health potion')
assert potion is not None, 'Should find health potion'
assert potion['name'] == 'health potion', 'Item name should match'
assert potion['price'] == 50, 'Potion price should be 50'
assert merchant.get_item('magic wand') is None, 'Should return None for unknown item'

# Test NPCManager
mgr = NPCManager()
assert hasattr(mgr, '_npcs'), 'NPCManager should have _npcs dict'

# Test load_npcs
definitions = [
    {
        'name': 'Blacksmith',
        'dialogue': {'hello': 'Need something forged?'},
        'inventory': [{'name': 'steel sword', 'price': 300}],
        'is_merchant': True
    },
    {
        'name': 'Old Man',
        'dialogue': {'hello': 'Back in my day...', 'wisdom': 'Save often.'},
        'inventory': [],
        'is_merchant': False
    }
]
mgr.load_npcs(definitions)

assert mgr.get_npc('Blacksmith') is not None, 'Should find Blacksmith'
assert mgr.get_npc('Old Man') is not None, 'Should find Old Man'
assert mgr.get_npc('Ghost') is None, 'Should return None for unknown NPC'

# Test interact
resp = mgr.interact('Blacksmith', 'hello')
assert resp == 'Need something forged?', f'Expected greeting, got: {resp}'

resp2 = mgr.interact('Ghost', 'hello')
assert resp2 is None, 'interact with unknown NPC should return None'

# Test buy
success, msg, cost = mgr.buy('Blacksmith', 'steel sword', 500)
assert success is True, f'Buy should succeed with enough gold, got: {msg}'
assert cost == 300, f'Cost should be 300, got {cost}'
assert 'steel sword' in msg.lower() or 'Purchased' in msg, f'Message should mention item: {msg}'

# Test buy - not enough gold
success, msg, cost = mgr.buy('Blacksmith', 'steel sword', 100)
assert success is False, 'Buy should fail without enough gold'
assert cost == 0, 'Cost should be 0 on failure'

# Test buy - item not found
success, msg, cost = mgr.buy('Blacksmith', 'magic wand', 1000)
assert success is False, 'Buy should fail for unknown item'

# Test buy - not a merchant
success, msg, cost = mgr.buy('Old Man', 'anything', 1000)
assert success is False, 'Buy should fail for non-merchant NPC'

# Test sell
success, msg, earnings = mgr.sell('Blacksmith', 'old sword', 100)
assert success is True, f'Sell should succeed with merchant, got: {msg}'
assert earnings == 50, f'Sell price should be half (50), got {earnings}'

# Test sell - not a merchant
success, msg, earnings = mgr.sell('Old Man', 'old sword', 100)
assert success is False, 'Sell should fail for non-merchant NPC'
assert earnings == 0, 'Earnings should be 0 on failure'
acceptance_criteria: NPC stores name, dialogue dict, inventory list, and is_merchant bool
NPC.get_response() returns dialogue for known keywords or a default message
NPC.get_item() finds items by name in the inventory
NPCManager.load_npcs() creates NPC objects from definition dicts
NPCManager.interact() returns dialogue responses or None for unknown NPCs
NPCManager.buy() checks merchant status, item existence, and player gold
NPCManager.sell() calculates half-price and checks merchant status
hints: Use dialogue=None in __init__ and set self.dialogue = dialogue or {} to avoid mutable defaults.
In get_item(), use: next((item for item in self.inventory if item['name'] == item_name), None)
In buy(), chain checks: NPC exists -> is_merchant -> item found -> enough gold.
Sell price is integer division: base_price // 2.
